data.title: FDA rejects Lexicon's Zynquista for diabetes, kidney disease
data.description:
Lexicon Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:LXRX) said the FDA has declined to approve its drug Zynquista, also known as sotagliflozin, as an adjunct to insulin therapy for glycemic control in adults with type 1 diabetes and chronic kidney disease, or CKD, issuing the company a Complete Response Letter.

"This expected communication from the FDA aligns with the company’s previously disclosed strategic decision to discontinue launch preparations for Zynquista and focus solely on its clinical development pipeline," Lexicon said in a statement.

Lexicon said that its near-term focus will be on its drug candidate LX9211 for diabetic neuropathic pain, with topline Phase 2b data expected in Q1 2025.

In November, Lexicon announced it was winding down commercial operations to conserve cash and focus on its pipeline after receiving a letter from the FDA noting deficiencies in its Zynquista application.

data.title: EMERGE appoints interim CFO
data.description:
• EMERGE Commerce (TSXV:ECOM:CA) announced appointment of Dasha Enenko, CPA, CA as interim chief financial officer and corporate secretary, effective February 2025.
  • Dasha will succeed Kyle Burt-Gerrans, who will be stepping down to pursue other opportunities. Kyle will remain with the company until January 31, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Peoples Bancorp agrees to sell its assets, liabilities to Hanscom Federal Credit Union
data.description:
Peoples Bancorp (OTCPK:PEBC) has agreed to sell the majority of its assets and liabilities to Hanscom Federal Credit Union in an all-cash deal, the companies said Friday.

Unanimously approved by the boards of both organizations, the transaction represents Hanscom's entry into Maryland and expands its business offering with Peoples Bank's (OTCPK:PEBC) insurance agency business, Fleetwood Insurance Group.

Peoples (OTCPK:PEBC) will iquidate the remaining assets and liabilities not purchased by Hanscom, and will distribute the remaining cash to its shareholders in conjunction with, or prior to, the dissolution of the holding company and the bank. Hanscom also plans to retain all employees of Peoples Bank after the acquisition.

With the deal, expected to be completed in the second half of 2025, Hanscom will have assets totaling about $2.1B and an expanded reach with 23 branches across Massachusetts, Maryland and Virginia.

data.title: Stran & Company announces Nasdaq delisting letter
data.description:
• Stran & Company (NASDAQ:SWAG) announced that on December 17, 2024, it received a letter from Nasdaq issuing a delisting determination.
  • Nasdaq had notified the company on June 21, 2024, August 23, 2024 and November 21, 2024 that the company had not filed its quarterly reports for the periods ended March 31, 2024, June 30, 2024, and September 30, 2024.
  • The company intends to timely submit a request for a hearing and for an extended stay before a Hearings Panel.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: NiCAN announces settlement of debt for shares
data.description:
• NiCAN Limited (TSXV:NICN:CA) said on Friday that it has negotiated the settlement of $269,799 related to drilling services provided to the company in 2023 in consideration for the issuance of 300,000 common shares of the company.
  • The common shares issued pursuant to the debt settlement will be subject to a statutory hold period, which will expire four months and one day from the date of closing of the debt settlement.

data.title: Jiayin Group announces purchase of commercial property
data.description:
• Jiayin Group (NASDAQ:JFIN) on Friday said it entered into a definitive agreement to purchase certain commercial property located in Shanghai, China, of approximately 43,500 square meters for total cash consideration of approximately RMB1.35 billion.
  • The commercial property will primarily be used as the company's new headquarters.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Rumble announces $775M strategic investment from Tether; shares jump
data.description:
• Rumble (NASDAQ:RUM) said on Friday that it has entered into a definitive agreement for a strategic investment of $775 million from Tether.
  • The company will use $250 million of the proceeds to support growth initiatives and the remaining proceeds to fund a self tender offer for up to 70 million of its class A common stock, at the same price ($7.50 per share) as Tether’s investment.
  • Following the completion of the transaction, Chris Pavlovski, Rumble’s Chairman and CEO, will retain his controlling stake in the company.
  • Tether will own a minority position in Rumble’s outstanding common stock but will not have the right to designate any members of the board.
  • The investment and the tender offer are expected to close in the first quarter of 2025.
  • Rumble (RUM) shares were up more than 28% in post-market trading.

data.title: Exicure announces shareholders approve the $8.7 million equity financing
data.description:
• Exicure (NASDAQ:XCUR) on Friday said the stockholders approved a second investment of $8.7 million from HiTron Systems.
  • The investment is expected to close within a few days depending on regulatory approvals, the company said.
  • Exicure's board of directors also changed the authorized number of directors comprising the board to nine directors, and appointed four new directors to the board and two officers to the company.
  • The total investment from HiTron will be $10 million after the initial investment of $1.3 million closed in November, said the company.
  • The share issuance price for HiTron is $3 per share per the common stock purchase agreement signed in early November.
  • Following the closing of the second investment, HiTron will become Exicure's largest shareholder, owning over 50% of the company.
  • HiTron completed the acquisition of Exicure's management rights through the appointment of board members on December 17, 2024.
  • XCUR -0.05% after hours to $18.66.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Fed's Barr said to explore legal options against potential removal by Trump
data.description:
Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michael Barr has sought legal advance to assess his options in the face of potential efforts by President-elect Donald Trump to remove him from his position, according to a media report published on Friday.

President Joe Biden had nominated Barr in 2022 to take the position of Wall Street's top cop.

In recent weeks, Barr consulted law firm Arnold & Porter in a personal capacity for legal guidance, Reuters reported, citing two sources familiar with the matter. His has told Congress he plans to serve out his term, which expires in July 2026.

The news comes after Trump said On Dec. 8 he won't try to remove Fed Chair Jerome Powell before his term ends in May 2026. Powell said a month ago that he will not resign, even if Trump were to ask him to, or that he can be fired by the incoming president. “Not permitted under the law,” Powell said at the time.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Panther Minerals clarifies option agreement for Huber Heights property
data.description:
• Panther Minerals (OTCPK:GLIOF) on Friday said it has entered into the agreement with an arm’s length purchaser for the Huber Heights Property located in Nevada, USA.
  • The agreement grants the purchaser the exclusive right to acquire up to a 100% undivided interest in the property over a two-year period, subject to a 2% net smelter royalty.
  • The purchaser will make a cash payment of C$150,000 within 45 days of the agreement’s execution, the company said.
  • Panther Minerals retains a 2% net smelter royalty on production from the property, with the purchaser holding an option to buy out the royalty for C$1 million.
  • GLIOF closed -10.8209% at $0.0239.
  • Source: Press Release
